summary_one_line: The 4-byte DeviceClass FOURCC at offset 0x0c must equal 'scnr' (0x73636e72, cmsSigInputClass) to take the winning switch arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The DeviceClass field at input offsets 0x0c-0x0f must hold the exact FOURCC 'scnr' (bytes 73 63 6e 72 = cmsSigInputClass). This is read into Icc->DeviceClass and switched on in validDeviceClass; only the 'scnr' value lands on the True side of case cmsSigInputClass at line 749.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed carries 73 63 6e 72 ('scnr') at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, exactly matching cmsSigInputClass, so the switch falls into the case at line 749 (W-only) and returns TRUE.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds carry 6d 6e 74 72 ('mntr', cmsSigDisplayClass) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, which dispatches to case cmsSigDisplayClass at line 750 (L hits T=10) and never reaches the cmsSigInputClass True side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer CMP on the 4-byte DeviceClass and writes a CMP_MAP bucket keyed by operand-distance; an input whose DeviceClass is Hamming-closer to 'scnr' than any prior input adds a new bucket and is retained, giving a gradient that guides mutation from 'mntr' toward 'scnr'. naive has no CMP introspection and must hit the exact 4-byte literal by blind havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case cmsSigInputClass arm of a switch on the profile DeviceClass signature.
The blocker line 749 is taken only by the winner role in the per-role overlay.
DeviceClass is read from the header and validated via validDeviceClass before acceptance.
The winner-resolving seed contains 'scnr' (73 63 6e 72) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f.
The loser-blocking seeds contain 'mntr' (6d 6e 74 72) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f.
Offsets 0x0c-0x0e differ decisively between W ('s','c','n') and L ('m','n','t').
All loser seeds dispatch to the cmsSigDisplayClass arm instead, hitting its True side 10 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 750  T=0 F=1  T=10 F=0  case cmsSigDisplayClass:

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile adds CMP_MAP buckets keyed by operand-distance, retaining inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only edge counters and no CMP introspection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile resolved 10/10 trials while naive blocked 10/10.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage verification is mandatory only for claimed_mechanism=I2SRandReplace; the claimed mechanism here is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile distance feedback), so no I2S dash-row check applies. The MECHANISM CONTEXT for value_profile explicitly states 'No I2SRandReplace' and that observed mutation_ops are 'havoc/token names only', consistent with a CMP_MAP-driven, not I2S-driven, win.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on an exact 4-byte DeviceClass literal where value_profile shows no faster resolution than naive (no dose-response advantage), indicating the CMP_MAP distance gradient confers no benefit toward the FOURCC match.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is shown, so the 'scnr' requirement is inferred from a single W example versus five consistent L examples rather than a population of winners.
